title: centaurea argentea subsp. chionantha
intro: Centaurea argentea subsp. chionantha, also known as mountain cornflower, is a flowering plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae) that typically thrives in dry, rocky areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Centaurea argentea subsp. chionantha include its potential to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall well-being and to soothe various health complaints.
